Title: File Section — Upload Button Disabled After 5th File Uploaded (Max Limit)
Section: Configurable Products > File Section > Upload Limits
Preconditions:
[PRE:CLEAR_SESSION]
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
User logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}. Configurable product with optional File section accessible at {{FRONT_URL}}/products-with-options/configurable-caps-shirts/physical (Hoodie Base, optional File section). Upload fixtures available: Logo1.png, Netflix-25.jpg, newier.pdf, Test.xlsx, large_test.png.
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}}/products-with-options/configurable-caps-shirts/physical
[WAIT] Product configuration widget visible
[SCROLL] Scroll to File upload section
[ASSERT] File upload button visible and enabled
[ACT] Upload file 1: Logo1.png via file upload control
[WAIT] File 1 appears in uploaded files list
[ASSERT] Upload button still enabled after file 1
[ACT] Upload file 2: Netflix-25.jpg via file upload control
[WAIT] File 2 appears in uploaded files list
[ACT] Upload file 3: newier.pdf via file upload control
[WAIT] File 3 appears in uploaded files list
[ACT] Upload file 4: Test.xlsx via file upload control
[WAIT] File 4 appears in uploaded files list
[ACT] Upload file 5: large_test.png via file upload control
[WAIT] File 5 appears in uploaded files list — 5 files total
[ASSERT] Upload button is now disabled or hidden (max 5 reached)
[ACT] Delete file 3 (newier.pdf) from uploaded files list
[WAIT] File 3 removed; 4 files remain
[ASSERT] Upload button is re-enabled after deletion
Assertions:
[DOM] Upload button disabled or hidden when 5 files are present
[DOM] Upload button re-enabled after deleting one file (4 remain)
[STATE] File list shows exactly 5 files at max limit
[STATE] File list shows exactly 4 files after deletion
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No JS errors during upload sequence
[NETWORK] No 4xx/5xx on file upload requests
[API] Each upload returns success response
Failure_Signals: Spinner visible >5s on any upload; upload button not disabled at 5 files; upload button not re-enabled after deletion; console TypeError during upload
Cleanup: Remove all uploaded files from section; navigate away from product page

Title: Text Section — Max Length Enforced + Live Counter Verified (Required Text, 30-Char Limit)
Section: Configurable Products > Text Section > Validation
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
User logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}. Product AGENT-TEST-RING-TXT-CFG-20260327 has a required Text section with maxLength 30. PR #2263 deployed: live character counter visible as 'N / M' format, rendered by vc-input-details, associated via aria-describedby. Product URL at {{FRONT_URL}} in /products-with-options/.
Steps:
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}} — navigate to product AGENT-TEST-RING-TXT-CFG-20260327
[WAIT] Configuration widget visible
[SCROLL] Scroll to required Text section
[ASSERT] Text input field visible; character counter element visible showing '0 / 30' (empty state — note: space on each side of slash, e.g. '0 / 30' not '0/30')
[ASSERT] Input element has aria-describedby attribute linking to counter element (DOM check: input aria-describedby === counter element id) — aria-describedby is ALWAYS-ON regardless of count
[ASSERT] Counter element at rest (count 0): does NOT have role=status or aria-live (these are applied only at-cap; plain div below cap)
[ACT] Type exactly 30 characters into text field: 'Exactly thirty characters here!'
[WAIT] Counter updates reactively
[ASSERT] Counter shows '30 / 30'; counter element has BEM modifier class vc-input-details__counter--limit (at-cap visual emphasis, color #DE3131)
[ASSERT] Counter element at cap has role=status and aria-live='polite' (conditionally applied at-cap)
[ACT] Attempt to type one more character (31st character: 'X')
[ASSERT] 31st character not accepted (input capped at 30); counter still shows '30 / 30'
[ACT] Clear field; type text exceeding 30 chars by pasting: 'This text is way too long for this field!'
[WAIT] Counter updates
[ASSERT] Text truncated to 30 characters; counter shows '30 / 30' with vc-input-details__counter--limit class
[ACT] Clear field; type 1 character: 'A'
[WAIT] Counter updates
[ASSERT] Counter shows '1 / 30'; vc-input-details__counter--limit class absent; role/aria-live not present on counter
[ACT] Click 'Add to Cart'
[WAIT] Success notification
Assertions:
[STATE] Text field enforces 30-character maximum via HTML maxlength attribute
[STATE] 30 characters accepted exactly; counter shows '30 / 30'; vc-input-details__counter--limit applied; role=status + aria-live=polite conditionally active
[STATE] 31st character rejected or trimmed; counter does not exceed '30 / 30'
[STATE] 1 character accepted; counter shows '1 / 30'; no at-cap class; no role/aria-live on counter
[DOM] Character counter element present; shows 'N / M' format with spaces around slash (e.g., '0 / 30', '15 / 30', '30 / 30')
[DOM] aria-describedby on input references counter element id — always-on link
[DOM] At-cap (30/30): counter has class vc-input-details__counter--limit (BEM modifier, color ≈ #DE3131); role=status and aria-live='polite' conditionally present
[DOM] Below cap: counter is plain div — no role=status, no aria-live, no vc-input-details__counter--limit class
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No JS errors on limit enforcement or counter update
[NETWORK] No API call made for over-length input attempts
[API] addItem call includes text value (trimmed to limit); errors[] empty
Failure_Signals: 31st character accepted without indication; counter element absent from DOM; counter not updating reactively on keypress; counter format '0/30' without spaces (space-format regression); vc-input-details__counter--limit absent at 30/30; aria-describedby missing on input; vc-input-details__counter--limit present below cap (applied too early); add-to-cart blocked by 1-char input
Cleanup: Remove item from cart after test
References: VCST-4806; VCST-4928; PR #107 (XCart backend validation); PR #2235 (frontend MAX_LENGTH from section); PR #2263 (live counter + aria-describedby); Synced: VCST-4928 / PR #2263 (2026-04-20)

[WAIT] Success notification

Title: Text Counter — Accessibility: aria-describedby Always-On + Conditional role=status at Cap; WCAG 2.1 AA 1.3.1 + 4.1.3 via axe
Section: Configurable Products > Text Section > Character Counter > Accessibility
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
User logged in as {{USER_EMAIL}}. Product AGENT-TEST-RING-TXT-CFG-20260327 has required Text section with maxLength 30. PR #2263 actual a11y model: aria-describedby on input is ALWAYS-ON (present at rest and at cap); role=status and aria-live=polite on counter are CONDITIONAL — applied only when vc-input-details__counter--limit class is active (at-cap). axe run in both states. WCAG 2.1 AA 1.3.1 satisfied via always-on aria-describedby; 4.1.3 satisfied via conditional role=status at cap.
Steps:
--- STOREFRONT ---
[NAV] {{FRONT_URL}} — navigate to product AGENT-TEST-RING-TXT-CFG-20260327
[WAIT] Configuration widget visible
[SCROLL] Scroll to required Text section
[ACT] browser_evaluate: locate the text input in the Text section
[ASSERT] Input element has an accessible name (via aria-label, label[for], or aria-labelledby) that includes the section name — WCAG 1.3.1
[ASSERT] Input element has aria-describedby attribute referencing the counter element id — ALWAYS-ON at rest (field empty, count = 0)
[ASSERT] Counter at rest (count 0): NO role=status, NO aria-live (plain div; conditional attrs not yet applied)
[ACT] browser_evaluate: run axe-core on configuration widget at rest (empty field) — check wcag2a + wcag2aa
  Alternatively: use Chrome DevTools accessibility panel to confirm input/counter relationship
[ASSERT] axe at-rest state: 0 critical/serious violations (wcag2a + wcag2aa)
[ACT] Type 30 characters to reach cap
[WAIT] Counter updates to '30 / 30'; vc-input-details__counter--limit class applied
[ASSERT] Counter at cap: role=status NOW present (conditionally applied with --limit class)
[ASSERT] Counter at cap: aria-live='polite' NOW present (conditionally applied) — screen reader will announce '30 / 30'
[ASSERT] Counter text content is '30 / 30' (spaced format; live region update triggers announcement)
[ASSERT] Input aria-describedby still present at cap (always-on — not removed when cap reached)
[ACT] browser_evaluate: re-run axe on configuration widget at cap state
[ASSERT] axe at-cap state: 0 critical/serious violations (wcag2a + wcag2aa)
Assertions:
[DOM] Input has accessible name — WCAG 1.3.1
[DOM] Input aria-describedby references counter element id — always-on; present at rest AND at cap
[DOM] Counter at rest (count < max): plain div — no role, no aria-live
[DOM] Counter at cap (count = max = 30): role=status and aria-live='polite' conditionally active; vc-input-details__counter--limit class present
[DOM] axe: 0 critical/serious violations in both at-rest and at-cap states (wcag2a + wcag2aa)
[STATE] WCAG 1.3.1 satisfied by always-on aria-describedby (constraint communicated programmatically at all times)
[STATE] WCAG 4.1.3 satisfied by conditional role=status + aria-live at cap (status message exposed to AT when limit reached)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] No axe violation errors in console (axe-core injected or DevTools panel)
[NETWORK] No 4xx/5xx during page load or accessibility audit
Failure_Signals: axe reporting WCAG 1.3.1 violation (aria-describedby missing or broken); input missing accessible name; aria-describedby absent at rest; aria-describedby removed at cap; role=status present at rest instead of only at-cap (over-application); role=status absent at cap (under-application); aria-live set to 'off' or 'assertive' instead of 'polite'; counter format '0/30' without spaces; Coffee theme color contrast <4.5:1 for counter text (WCAG 1.4.3)
Cleanup: Navigate away from PDP; no cart cleanup needed
References: VCST-4928; PR #2263 (conditional role=status + aria-live=polite at-cap; always-on aria-describedby); WCAG 2.1 AA criteria: 1.3.1 Info and Relationships, 4.1.3 Status Messages; Technique: Error Guessing (a11y misimplementation patterns); Note: Only Coffee theme is WCAG-compliant per memory/feedback_a11y_coffee_only.md — test on Coffee theme
